questions: The brief suggests matching `lockfile.*needs to be updated` OR `lockfile is out of date`. The actual production message we have evidence for is `The lockfile at \`uv.lock\` needs to be updated, but \`--locked\` was provided.` — is matching `lockfile.*needs to be updated` (case-insensitive) sufficient, or do you also want to OR-in `lockfile is out of date` defensively even though we haven't confirmed `uv` ever emits it?

If `uv lock` succeeds but the recovery `uv sync` (no `--locked`) fails, the brief says hard-fail. Currently `set -e` is re-enabled before auto-heal runs, so any non-zero rc aborts the script with the existing ERR trap. Acceptable, or do you want a more specific log line like `❌ Auto-heal recovery sync failed; aborting` before the implicit abort?
Should the recovery `uv lock` also pass `--extra cuda124`, or is `--python 3.10` alone sufficient (matching the brief's literal command)?

success_criteria.criterion: The `else` branch in `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/worker_startup.template.sh` captures `uv sync --locked` exit code (uses `set +e`/`set -e` around it) instead of letting `set -e` immediately abort.

On lockfile-drift detection (`grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated'` matches the captured output), the script runs `"$UV_BIN" lock --python 3.10` followed by `"$UV_BIN" sync --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` (no `--locked`) and only writes the sentinel after both succeed.
After auto-heal, the sentinel hash written reflects the regenerated `uv.lock` (i.e. `UV_LOCK_SHA256` and `EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH` are recomputed between `uv lock` and `printf ... > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"`).
A clearly distinct, greppable warning line containing `Lockfile drift detected` is emitted to `$LOG_FILE` before the auto-heal commands run.
If the captured sync output does NOT match the lockfile-drift marker, the script exits with the original non-zero exit code (does not swallow the failure).
A new test in `tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py` asserts the drift marker grep, both auto-heal commands, the post-recovery sentinel write ordering, and the warning line.
Existing test `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` still passes (sentinel-skip fast-path and `'"$UV_BIN" sync --locked'` ordering preserved).
Style matches the existing template — bash-3 compatible, no new top-level dependencies, no Sentry/Slack alerting added.
Idempotency: re-running the startup script on a healthy volume after auto-heal still skips via the sentinel on the next boot (sentinel matches recomputed inputs hash).

assumptions: Marker pattern `grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated'` is sufficient — it matches the only failure message we have production evidence for.
Recovery `uv lock` only needs `--python 3.10` (matching the brief literally); cuda124 is an extra for sync, not for lock resolution.

The recovery `uv sync` (without `--locked`) appends to `$LOG_FILE` directly — same pattern as the rest of the template — and relies on the restored `set -e` + ERR trap to hard-fail on a real resolution problem.

Existing test `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` will continue to pass because the new structure keeps `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` before, `'"$UV_BIN" sync --locked'` in the middle, and `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` after — only now inside the success arm of the rc branch.
No changes are needed in `startup_script.py` (the renderer); the template is loaded verbatim and the placeholders we touch are unchanged.
